prompts.text: Words to use throughout: 'altogether', 'total', 'more than', 'fewer than', 'equal'. Model them naturally: 'Group A has 3 and group B has 2 — altogether that's 5.'
Name the + and − action each time: 'You added one more — now there are four. One more than three is four.' This builds one-more/one-less language in a concrete context.
Core question to return to: 'Which group has more? How do you know?' — pushes beyond guessing to reasoning about quantity.
Challenge: 'Can you make both groups equal — the same amount in each? What's the total when they match?'
